abstract.shortdesc: Use these instructions to configure the client to connect to z/OS by using Transport Layer Security (TLS) client authentication with a locally installed PKCS12 file generated from a RACF certificate.

section.title: Host TLS configuration
section.p: Before you configure TLS on the client, you must configure the z/OS server for TLS. The instructions for configuring the host are in the Host Configuration Guide in ssl.properties, the RSE encrypted communication. This configuration is done by a z/OS system administrator with the assistance of a security administrator. If you are unsure if the z/OS system has been configured for TLS, check with your z/OS system administrator.

section.title: Configuring a new z/OS connection for TLS
section.p:
To define a new connection to a z/OS system that is configured for TLS, do these steps:
Click Next.
On the Connection Configuration page, specify these options:
Daemon Port: Specify a valid port number. Consult your z/OS system administrator for the port number to use.
Authentication method:Select certificate to use client certificate authentication.
Click Finish.
To configure the client certificate, follow the instructions in .

section.title: Configuring TLS on an existing z/OS connection
section.p:
If you already have a z/OS connection defined, and you want to change the connection properties to use TLS, do these steps:
In the Remote Systems view, right-click the connection name and select Properties.
In the navigation pane of the Properties window, select Connector Services.
Expand the entries in the Available Services list and click Launcher Properties.
In the Authentication method row of the Properties table, click the Value cell, then expand the drop-down list and select certificate.
Click Apply and Close.

ol.li: From the menu bar, click WindowPreferences.
In the navigation pane of the Properties window, select Client Certificates.

Specify these values on the Client Certificates Preferences page: Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) Provider: IBMJCE Keystore Type: PKCS12 hostIdMappings Object Identifier (OID): The OID value 1.3.18.0.2.18.1 can remain or be cleared so that there is no value. If the value is not cleared, the certificate must have this OID included. You can define multiple hostIdMappings if you need to access multiple z/OS systems or multiple applications, but only the first entry in the set of hostIdMappings is used. If the first entry is not the one that is authorized to the RSE server user ID, then the session fails with the error message checkCertificate:Invalid Certificate Exception: SERVAUTH Definition Error even if one of the additional entries maps to a valid SERVAUTH definition for the RSE server user ID.

To connect with the certificate, disconnect and then reconnect to the z/OS system. If the certificate is signed by its own CA or a self-signed certificate is used, then you are prompted to trust the certificate before connecting. If you are using a well-known CA, there is no prompt for the trust and the z/OS system is connected.
